text: Separation of noble gases by cryogenic distillation is energetically and economically costly, particularly when low concentrations of noble gases are present. Large number of MOFs comprising polarising sites in the cavities have been predicted to be potential Xe/Kr solid adsorbents but so far no detailed study has been reported on Xe-network interactions. Here we report an MOF SBMOF-2 with selective Xe adsorption over Kr at ambient conditions and gave Xe/Kr selectivity about 10 and Xe uptake of 27.07 wt% at 298 K. The single crystal X-ray diffraction analysis revealed Xe selectivity to pore structure where a particular set of cages constituted of phenyl rings surrounded by cavities rich in polar –OH species facilitating strong affinity toward polarizable Xe gas molecules. Breakthrough experiments with SBMOF-2 provided Xe/Kr separation have been carried out by employing a number of experimental and simulation techniques and revealed that for the first time the feasibility of Xe/Kr separation by SBMOF-2 adsorbent. Further the capacity and selectivity towards Xe/Kr adsorption on SBMOF-2 was calculated using breakthrough simulations and found that the performance of the adsorbent is close to the reported values for the unmodified MOFs like NiMOF-74 and Co formate.
